rows.what_you_get: A cheap, deterministic check that refuses a malformed tool call at the boundary — static parse first, then JSON-Schema validation, cheapest-first, before anything fires.
rows.witness: internal/preflight tests (units 47-51): rung-0 static parse + rung-1 schema check
gaps: Rung-2 dry-run and rung-3 sandbox probes are STUB (not built in v0.1)
Decode-time grammar-constrained generation requires owning the decode loop (deferred)
rows.durability_note: Rung-0/rung-1 are in-process stdlib parsing/JSON-Schema validation with zero external deps, tested on every commit.

rows.what_you_get: An in-process, model-free transform that repairs an arity-matched positional tool call into a named one without a model turn; unrepairable calls are denied with MISROUTE.
rows.witness: internal/grammar tests (units 52-57): positional repair, content-addressed grammar dedup
gaps: A repair rung inside preflight, not a standalone surface a person runs directly
Static grammar table only; unrepairable calls do not auto-learn a new grammar
rows.durability_note: Deterministic, in-process, stdlib-only; content-addressed dedup avoids duplicate repair overhead.

rows.what_you_get: A pipeline gate that grades the SHAPE of any text (repetition, repeated-line blocks, short-period tiling) against tunable thresholds and exits 1 when it is degenerate — catch a looping model in CI.
rows.first_command: echo "na na na na na na na" | go run ./cmd/fak answer-shape
gaps: A deterministic graded floor; a model-backed semantic detector is not built
Thresholds are tunable but there is no per-domain default calibration
rows.durability_note: Pure-Go stdlib-only, deterministic cross-platform; cross-checked on every turn against the kernel's own admit verdict.

rows.concept: Doctor (operator diagnostic over answer-shape + kernel admit)
rows.what_you_get: Pipe text in and get operator recommendations plus the real kernel admit verdict on the same bytes — the fak analogue of `dos doctor`, an off-path read-only health check.
rows.witness: cmd/fak doctor_test.go (exit-code + JSON contracts, kernel cross-check via ctxmmu.ScreenBytes)
gaps: Read-only, off the live agent loop (advisory only)
rows.durability_note: No side effects; cross-checks the live kernel admit verdict byte-for-byte; deterministic exit codes for CI gating.

rows.what_you_get: Lint code your agent produced for HARD parse/compile errors — Go/JSON in-process, Python/CUDA via their toolchains — and exit 1 on a real error, the write-time code check at the kernel boundary.
rows.witness: internal/codelint tests (Go/JSON parse-error, Python real-compile presence-gated)
gaps: Parse/compile tier only, not semantic checks (a deliberate zero-false-positive floor)
Honors no in-content ignore comment (the model must not switch the gate off)
rows.durability_note: Go/JSON check in-process via the stdlib (zero external deps); Python/CUDA degrade to no-opinion when absent; off the hot path by construction.

rows.concept: Write-scoped codelint verdict in the adjudicator
rows.what_you_get: An opt-in policy knob that refuses a whole-file write of unparseable Go/JSON with Deny(MALFORMED) + a bounded file:line:col witness before it lands — the in-kernel dual of the fleet's advisory lint.
gaps: Off by default (lint_writes manifest field); an existing floor is byte-for-byte unchanged
Go/JSON only on the decide path (Python/CUDA shell out, so they DEFER fail-open)
rows.durability_note: The adjudicator does not import codelint (that would put exec on the request path); the off-by-default knob preserves the ABI golden freeze.

rows.concept: Shared task record fold (collaborative task contract)
rows.what_you_get: An in-memory reference fold for a shared task record: a Store that applies user/agent patches against a base revision, auto-merges commuting writes, returns typed conflicts for stale ones, and publishes accepted events on a capability-floored a2achan topic so collaborators observe live updates.
gaps: Off the request path — it is the reference fold + scoped read projections, not a durable or networked task service
Replay is snapshot-based (Journal), not a claim that raw event rows alone rebuild state
rows.durability_note: Disaggregated artifact/note/body refs are admitted only when digest-shaped and carrying a deletion certificate; missing witnesses are quarantined without advancing the record.

rows.concept: Trajectory observability primitives (data plane + reference similarity + scorer seam)
rows.what_you_get: A typed, exportable per-turn Turn record folded from the kernel's lifecycle stream (FAK_TRAJECTORY=1), a dependency-free simhash similarity index for finding near-duplicate bad queries, and a pluggable Turn→Finding scorer registry — the substrate the trajectory-garden skill builds on.
rows.claims_section: Trajectory observability primitives (data plane + reference similarity + scorer seam)
gaps: The `fak traj similar|cluster|score|gc|export` toolkit exists over an exported corpus, but is not yet listed in docs/cli-reference.md as a first-class verb
simhash is a hashing-trick sketch (same-intent/different-vocabulary pairs score ~0.35), not a learned embedding
rows.durability_note: The Recorder is an abi.Emitter reading the OPEN Fields/Meta channels, so it adds no field to the frozen ABI; gc PROPOSES prune candidates, never deletes.
